title: Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22
description: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. (+3 related finding(s))
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether an Amazon EC2 security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. The control fails if the security group allows ingress from ‘0.0.0.0/0’ or ‘::/0’ to port 22. (+3 related finding(s))
what_the_fix_does: Restricts risky inbound security-group rules on unauthorized public ports.

findings.title: Security groups should only allow unrestricted incoming traffic for authorized ports
Security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 to port 22
Security groups should not allow unrestricted access to ports with high risk
EC2 security groups should not allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
